summary: Machine policy for MDM enrollment. Good for systems that should not be remotely managed through MDM, but it prevents new MDM enrollments for all users on the device.

setting.name: MDM Enrollment
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can allow the computer to enroll into a Mobile Device Management service for remote management. This policy decides whether new MDM enrollment is allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable MDM enrollment to stop devices from being enrolled into remote management unintentionally or to keep personal systems out of enterprise-style device management.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off prevents new device management enrollment, but it also blocks legitimate MDM onboarding for work or school environments.
setting.professional_notes: MDM.admx maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\MDM\DisableRegistration with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text says enabling the policy disables MDM enrollment for all users and does not unenroll existing enrollments.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: MDM enrollment remains enabled because the machine policy is not forcing it off.

allowed_values.label: MDM enrollment enabled
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and MDM enrollment is allowed for all users.

allowed_values.label: MDM enrollment disabled
allowed_values.meaning: New MDM enrollment is disabled for all users on the device. Existing MDM enrollments aren't removed by this policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableRegistration = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 client systems supporting the MDM policy

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text says MDM enrollment is enabled when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that may need work or school MDM onboarding
avoid_for: Personal devices that should never enroll into MDM
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want MDM enrollment available if the device needs it.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to make sure no new MDM enrollment can happen on the device.
tradeoffs: Work or school enrollment remains available.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for new MDM enrollment.

recommended_profiles.label: No MDM enrollment
avoid_for: Devices that need to join work or school management

states.rationale: Disable new MDM enrollment for all users on the device.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to prevent new MDM enrollment from being initiated on the device.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need the device to enroll into an MDM service for remote management.
tradeoffs: Existing MDM enrollments are not removed by this policy.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft MDM.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\MDM\DisableRegistration with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft MDM.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy disables MDM enrollment for all users and does not unenroll existing MDM enrollments, while disabled or not configured leaves MDM enrollment enabled.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableRegistration = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableRegistration policy
evidence.summary: MDM.admx shows DisableRegistration under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\MDM. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy disables MDM enrollment for all users, does not unenroll existing enrollments, and disabling it leaves MDM enrollment enabled.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact MDM enrollment policy surface, value semantics, and enrollment behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="MDM_MDM_DisplayName" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\MDM" valueName="DisableRegistration">; MDM.adml: If you enable this policy setting, MDM Enrollment will be disabled for all users. It will not unenroll existing MDM enrollments.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy disables new MDM enrollment without unenrolling existing devices. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-disableregistration-admx.
